system.description:
From the French word for “Knight” and “Fashion” (as in, following modern trends), the Chevalier de Mode Style of mounted combat is an attempt to blend the classical mounted lancer of old with the modern cavalry soldier armed with pistols. Fighters of the Chevalier Style are trained to charge with the lance, and once having struck with it, quickly switch to pistols to sow chaos among the ranks.

It is recommended that those who wish to learn the Chevalier de Mode Style also have the Born in the Saddle Boon (which grants a Bonus Die to non- combat riding checks). Most characters trained in the Chevalier de Mode Style should have the Soldier career, as this is a form of military training.

Style Detriments: You may only use the Chevalier de Mode Style while you are mounted. Likewise, because your feet are not on the ground, you cannot use Lunge or Footwork at all while using the Chevalier de Mode Style.

benefit.description: When charging with a lance from horseback, you do +1d6 Dmg instead of +1d3. After striking an opponent with the lance, you must discard it, but you may make a free Quick Draw attempt to grab a Horse Pistol. You do not have the typical penalty to Quick Draw a Horse Pistol from a saddle horn. When you strike with the butt of a Horse Pistol, you do 1d6 Dmg.
benefit.name: From the Old to the New

finalSecret.description: Everyone knows to fear the horseman who wades into battle with a thundering pistol in each hand, wreathed in smoke and surrounded by screams. When you Split Actions to fire two horse pistols as a Major Action, you gain a Terror Rating. The Terror Rating is equal to the number of attacks that hit (whether or not a Close Call was used). All enemies within Close Range must make a Daring test against your Terror Rating or Lose 1 Composure. Instead of losing Composure, Pawns will flee (roll 1d6+2 to see how many within Close Range do).
finalSecret.name: Horseman of Death

system.weaponry: Horse-Lance and Horse Pistols from horseback. Typically, the lance is discarded after a successful attack
